title: Introduction to the module on Array Operations

content.problemStatement:
Another useful class in Java is the **`Collections`** class. This includes various methods that can help us perform some more operations on **`ArrayList`**. Let's discuss a few that can be used while solving problems.

**Sort an ArrayList:**
```
import java.util.ArrayList;
import java.util.Collections; 

ArrayList seed-it = new ArrayList<();
codechef.add(45);
codechef.add(56);
codechef.add(23);

Collections.sort(seed-it); // Collection.sort() sorts the ArrayList numerically or alphabetically.
```

**Highest value/element of the ArrayList:**
```
Collections.max(seed-it); // Gives the highest value ArrayList seed-it.
```
**Lowest value/element of the ArrayList:**
```
Collections.min(seed-it); // Gives the lowest value ArrayList seed-it.
```

**Frequency of a value/element:**
```
Collections.frequency(seed-it, 23); // Counts the occurrences of the value "23" in the ArrayList.
```

**Swap elements:**
```
Collections.swap(seed-it, 0, 2); // Swaps the elements in the specified postion.
```
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ — the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
